item.title: Guide Labs debuts a new kind of interpretable LLM
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The company open-sourced an 8 billion parameter LLM, Steerling-8B, trained with a new architecture designed to make its actions easily interpretable.

item.title: Particle’s AI news app listens to podcasts for interesting clips so you you don’t have to
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: AI news app Particle can now pull in key moments from podcasts, letting readers instantly play short, relevant clips alongside related stories.

item.title: Spotify rolls out AI-powered Prompted Playlists to the U.K. and other markets
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Spotify continues to test its AI-powered “Prompted Playlists” feature, now rolling out the tool to Premium subscribers in the U.K., Ireland, Australia, and Sweden.

item.title: Finnish quantum unicorn IQM set to go public
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Finnish unicorn IQM plans to go public via a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) valuing the company at approximately $1.8 billion — joining the growing cohort of quantum computing companies listed on U.S. stock markets.

item.title: VPN flaws allowed Chinese hackers to compromise dozens of Ivanti customers, says report
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Chinese hackers allegedly broke into the network of an Ivanti subsidiary in 2021. The hackers exploited a backdoor in its VPN product, which allowed the hackers to gain access to 119 other unnamed organizations.

item.title: 5 days left to lock in the lowest TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket rates
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Five days to save up to $680 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket. These lowest rates of the year disappear on February 27 at 11:59 p.m. PT.

item.title: How AI agents could destroy the economy
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Citrini Research imagines a report from two years in the future, in which unemployment has doubled and the total value of the stock market has fallen by more than a third.

item.title: Defense Secretary summons Anthropic’s Amodei over military use of Claude
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has summoned Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei to the Pentagon for a tense discussion over the military's use of Claude. Hegseth has threatened to designate Anthropic a "supply chain risk."

item.title: Wispr Flow launches an Android app for AI-powered dictation
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The app can support translation in over 100 languages and can work across other apps. Wispr Flow said it has done an infrastructure rewrite that makes dictation 30% faster than before.

item.title: Apple might take a new approach to announcing its next products
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Apple has invited the tech press to a “special Apple experience” on March 4, but it might unfold a bit differently than the company's standard press event.

item.title: Can the creator economy stay afloat in a flood of AI slop?
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we debated what’s next for the creator economy, and whether there will be any room for the next generation of creators to stand out.

item.title: Bill Gurley says that right now, the worst thing you can do for your career is play it safe
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: "The number one thing is to get out of your head this ideal that gets passed around in the self-help world: 'go get a mentor,' and everyone runs out and cold calls someone that's ridiculously too high and unachievable, and it doesn't work."

item.title: Quantonation’s double-sized second fund shows quantum still has believers
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Quantonation Ventures, a venture firm investing in quantum and physics-based startups, has closed its oversubscribed second fund at €220 million, or approximately $260 million. That’s more than twice the size of its inaugural fund, and comes in addition to other signals that the quantum winter isn’t coming yet.

item.title: Trump says Netflix will face ‘consequences’ if it doesn’t fire board member Susan Rice
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Netflix board member Susan Rice had predicted that corporations that “take a knee” to Trump will be “held accountable” when Democrats return to power.

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Waymo makes its defense
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility — your central hub for news and insights on the future of transportation.

item.title: All the important news from the ongoing India AI Impact Summit
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: India is hosting a four-day AI Summit this week that will be attended by executives from major AI labs and Big Tech, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Nvidia, Microsoft, Google, and Cloudflare, as well as heads of state.

item.title: China’s brain-computer interface industry is racing ahead
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: China’s brain-computer interface industry is rapidly scaling from research to commercialization, driven by strong policy support, expanding clinical trials, and growing investor interest.

item.title: 6 days left to lock in the lowest TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 rates
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Super Early Bird pricing for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ends February 27 at 11:59 p.m. PT. That means you have just 6 days left to secure up to $680 of ticket savings.

item.title: The 9,000-pound monster I don’t want to give back
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: I thought: other than hotels that use SUVs like the Escalade IQL to ferry guests around, what kind of monster chooses a car like this?

item.title: Move over, Apple: Meet the alternative app stores available in the EU and elsewhere
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: A list of some of the alternative app stores iPhone users in the EU can try today.
